title: V3Number::setBit() thinks z = 3 instead of 2
body:
---


Author Name: **Todd Strader** (@toddstrader)
Original Redmine Issue: 971 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2015-09-30
Original Assignee: Todd Strader (@toddstrader)

---

Based on this comment:
```
     vector<uint32_t>    m_valueX;       // Each bit is true if it's X or Z, 10=z, 11=x

```

and bitIsZ(), I'm pretty sure setBit() is still wrong.  Unfortunately, I can't find anything that calls setBit with 2 or 3, so I don't know how to make a test for this.

I've posted a fix here:
https://github.com/toddstrader/verilator-setbit-fix
code_blocks: vector<uint32_t>    m_valueX;       // Each bit is true if it's X or Z, 10=z, 11=x
